dashboards_data.name: KOI Content Extension - Alerts Dashboard
dashboards_data.description: Alert-centric dashboard shipped by the KOI Content Extension pack. It reads the koi_koi_raw dataset produced by the Marketplace KOI pack v1.2.3 integration (13 commands). Widgets fall into two classes: those reading raw OCSF columns and JSON inside resources/observables, which work against any koi_koi_raw data; and those reading columns promoted by this pack's parsing rule (item_id, item_type, alert_hostname, device_last_user, device_os_obs, finding_title, mcp_type, mcp_transport, mcp_risk_level, mcp_risk_status, mcp_url, device_id). IMPORTANT: XSIAM parsing rules are applied at ingestion time only and already-stored events are never reprocessed, so every promoted column is NULL on the rows that were ingested before this pack was installed (~20,500 rows on the reference tenant). Those widgets stay empty until fresh KOI events arrive; set the dashboard time range to start at the install time to read them honestly. No widget depends on the modeling rule - the dashboard contains zero xdm.* references.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as total_alert
| view graph type = single subtype = standard yaxis = total_alert scale_threshold("#919291","#ff0082","400") dataunit = "Alert"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count_distinct(device_id) as devices
| view graph type = single subtype = standard yaxis = devices scale_threshold("#0054f5") dataunit = "Devices"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count_distinct(item_id) as items
| view graph type = single yaxis = items scale_threshold("#0054f5") dataunit = "Items"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| comp count_distinct(item_id) as mcp_servers
| view graph type = single yaxis = mcp_servers scale_threshold("#0054f5") dataunit = "MCP Servers"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter item_risk_status = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk_status"), null))
| filter item_risk_status != null
| alter assessed = if(item_risk_status = "completed", 1, 0)
| comp avg(assessed) as cov
| alter coverage_pct = multiply(cov, 100)
| view graph type = single yaxis = coverage_pct scale_threshold("#00cc88") dataunit = "%"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| filter item_type = "mcp_server" and mcp_risk_status = "pending"
| comp count_distinct(item_id) as pending_mcp
| view graph type = single yaxis = pending_mcp scale_threshold("#ff8c00") dataunit = "MCP Servers"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as alerts_by_severity by severity
| sort desc alerts_by_severity
| view graph type = pie subtype = full header = "Alerts by Severity" xaxis = severity yaxis = alerts_by_severity

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as alerts by item_type
| view graph type = pie subtype = donut header = "Alerts by Item Type" xaxis = item_type yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as alerts by risk_level
| sort desc alerts
| view graph type = pie header = "Alerts by Risk Level" xaxis = risk_level yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| bin _time span = 1d
| comp count() as alerts by _time, severity
| sort asc _time
| view graph type = area subtype = stacked header = "Severity Trend" xaxis = _time yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_severity = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.severity")
| filter finding_severity != null
| comp count() as findings by finding_severity
| sort desc findings
| view graph type = pie subtype = donut header = "Finding Severity Mix" xaxis = finding_severity yaxis = findings

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_name = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.finding_name")
| filter finding_name != null
| comp count() as findings by finding_name
| sort desc findings
| limit 15
| view graph type = column header = "Top Finding Types" xaxis = finding_name yaxis = findings

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_name = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.finding_name")
| filter finding_name in ("Unverified Publisher", "Individual Publisher", "Publisher Has Only One Item", "Publisher Low Install Count", "Low Install Count", "Publisher Email in Compromised List", "Publisher Compromised", "Unpopular GitHub Repository")
| comp count() as findings by finding_name
| sort desc findings
| view graph type = pie subtype = donut header = "Publisher Trust Signals" xaxis = finding_name yaxis = findings

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_name = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.finding_name")
| filter finding_name in ("Broad Host Permissions", "Code Execution Permissions", "Network Interception Permissions", "Identity & Authentication Permissions")
| comp count() as findings by finding_name
| sort desc findings
| view graph type = column header = "Risky Permission Findings" xaxis = finding_name yaxis = findings

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_severity = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.severity")
| filter finding_severity != null
| bin _time span = 1d
| comp count() as findings by _time, finding_severity
| sort asc _time
| view graph type = area subtype = stacked header = "Findings Over Time by Severity" xaxis = _time yaxis = findings series = finding_severity

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter risk_score = to_number(coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk"), null)))
| filter risk_score != null
| alter risk_band = if(risk_score < 2, "0-2", risk_score < 4, "2-4", risk_score < 6, "4-6", risk_score < 8, "6-8", "8-10")
| comp count() as items by risk_band
| sort asc risk_band
| view graph type = column header = "Risk Score Distribution" xaxis = risk_band yaxis = items

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter package_name = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.package_name"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.package_name"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.package_name"), null))
| filter package_name != null
| comp count() as alerts by package_name
| sort desc alerts
| limit 15
| view graph type = column header = "Top Packages by Alerts" xaxis = package_name yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter labels_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].labels"), null))
| arrayexpand labels_arr
| alter category = json_extract_scalar(labels_arr, "$")
| filter category != null
| comp count() as items by category
| sort desc items
| limit 15
| view graph type = column header = "Top Item Categories" xaxis = category yaxis = items

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter labels_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].labels"), null))
| arrayexpand labels_arr
| alter category = json_extract_scalar(labels_arr, "$")
| filter category != null
| comp count() as alerts by category, severity
| sort desc alerts
| limit 40
| view graph type = column subtype = stacked header = "Category by Severity" xaxis = category yaxis = alerts series = severity

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter marketplace = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.marketplace"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.marketplace"), null))
| filter marketplace != null
| bin _time span = 1d
| comp count() as alerts by _time, marketplace
| sort asc _time
| view graph type = area subtype = stacked header = "Marketplace Trend" xaxis = _time yaxis = alerts series = marketplace

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as alerts by device_os_obs, severity
| sort desc alerts
| view graph type = column subtype = stacked header = "Severity by OS" xaxis = device_os_obs yaxis = alerts series = severity

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as alerts by device_last_user
| sort desc alerts
| limit 10
| view graph type = pie subtype = full xaxis = device_last_user yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as alerts by alert_hostname, severity
| sort desc alerts
| limit 15
| view graph type = column subtype = stacked header = "Device Severity Concentration" xaxis = alert_hostname yaxis = alerts series = severity

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| comp count() as alerts by mcp_type
| view graph type = pie subtype = full header = "MCP: Local vs Remote" xaxis = mcp_type yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| comp count() as alerts by mcp_transport
| view graph type = pie subtype = donut header = "MCP Transport" xaxis = mcp_transport yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| comp count() as alerts by mcp_risk_level
| sort desc alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| comp count() as alerts by item_id
| sort desc alerts
| limit 10
| view graph type = funnel show_callouts = `true` show_callouts_names = `true` show_percentage = `false` xaxis = item_id yaxis = alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| bin _time span = 1d
| comp count() as mcp_alerts by _time
| sort asc _time
| view graph type = area header = "MCP Detections Over Time" xaxis = _time yaxis = mcp_alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| fields _time, finding_title, severity, risk_level, item_id, item_type, alert_hostname, device_last_user
| sort desc _time

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server" and severity in ("Critical", "High")
| fields _time, item_id, mcp_type, mcp_transport, mcp_risk_level, mcp_url, alert_hostname, device_last_user
| sort desc _time

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| comp count() as alerts by item_id
| sort desc alerts

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count_distinct(item_id) as items
| view graph type = single yaxis = items scale_threshold("#0054f5") dataunit = "Items"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as alerts_by_severity by severity
| sort desc alerts_by_severity
| view graph type = pie subtype = full header = "Alerts by Severity" xaxis = severity yaxis = alerts_by_severity

widgets_data.title: Koi Item Risk Level
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Item risk only. MCP risk level has its own widget ("Koi MCP Risk Level").

widgets_data.title: Koi Top Item Categories
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Categories are item `labels[]`; MCP resources carry no labels (0/18).

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter labels_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].labels"), null))
| arrayexpand labels_arr
| alter category = json_extract_scalar(labels_arr, "$")
| filter category != null
| comp count() as items by category
| sort desc items
| limit 15
| view graph type = column header = "Top Item Categories" xaxis = category yaxis = items

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| comp count() as total_alert
| view graph type = single subtype = standard yaxis = total_alert scale_threshold("#919291","#ff0082","400") dataunit = "Alert"

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server" and severity in ("Critical", "High")
| fields _time, item_id, mcp_type, mcp_transport, mcp_risk_level, mcp_url, alert_hostname, device_last_user
| sort desc _time

dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts" and item_type = "mcp_server"
| comp count_distinct(item_id) as mcp_servers
| view graph type = single yaxis = mcp_servers scale_threshold("#0054f5") dataunit = "MCP Servers"

widgets_data.title: Koi AI-Risk Findings by Marketplace
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Finding-name literals live-verified: "Uses Third Party AI Model" 5, "Remote Dynamic Dependency" 2, "LLM Derived Code Execution" 1, "Tools Invokable Without Authentication" 1, "Vulnerable to Prompt Injection" 1 (90d).

widgets_data.title: Koi Items With CVEs
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. FINDING D2 resolved: `contains "Vulnerability"` matches live values "Contains Vulnerability (CVSS High)" (12) and "Contains Vulnerability (CVSS Critical)" (4) = 16 findings in 90 days. Note it deliberately does NOT match the "Vulnerable to ..." family (Remote Code Execution 4, MITM 1, Prompt Injection 1), which are behavioural findings, not CVEs.

widgets_data.title: Koi Risky Permissions
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Live-verified: "Broad Host Permissions" 1, "Code Execution Permissions" 1, "Network Interception Permissions" 1. "Identity & Authentication Permissions" returns 0 in 90 days - kept in the IN-list as forward coverage; it costs nothing and simply contributes no bar.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_name = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.finding_name")
| filter finding_name in ("Broad Host Permissions", "Code Execution Permissions", "Network Interception Permissions", "Identity & Authentication Permissions")
| comp count() as findings by finding_name
| sort desc findings
| view graph type = column header = "Risky Permission Findings" xaxis = finding_name yaxis = findings

widgets_data.title: Koi Publisher Trust Signals
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Live-verified: "Individual Publisher" 59, "Publisher Email in Compromised List" 48, "Unpopular GitHub Repository" 3, "Publisher Low Install Count" 2, "Publisher Compromised" 2, "Low Install Count" 1. "Unverified Publisher" and "Publisher Has Only One Item" return 0 in 90 days - kept as forward coverage.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter findings_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].data.findings.findings"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].data.findings.findings"), null))
| arrayexpand findings_arr
| alter finding_name = json_extract_scalar(findings_arr, "$.finding_name")
| filter finding_name in ("Unverified Publisher", "Individual Publisher", "Publisher Has Only One Item", "Publisher Low Install Count", "Low Install Count", "Publisher Email in Compromised List", "Publisher Compromised", "Unpopular GitHub Repository")
| comp count() as findings by finding_name
| sort desc findings
| view graph type = pie subtype = donut header = "Publisher Trust Signals" xaxis = finding_name yaxis = findings

Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Device fields likewise come from `resources[.type = "device"]`.

widgets_data.title: Koi Risk Score Distribution
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Item risk score only. `data.risk` is present on 265/296 item resources and on only 2/18 MCP resources; including those 2 would have skewed the bands.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter risk_score = to_number(coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk"), null)))
| filter risk_score != null
| alter risk_band = if(risk_score < 2, "0-2", risk_score < 4, "2-4", risk_score < 6, "4-6", risk_score < 8, "6-8", "8-10")
| comp count() as items by risk_band
| sort asc risk_band
| view graph type = column header = "Risk Score Distribution" xaxis = risk_band yaxis = items

widgets_data.title: Koi Top Packages by Alerts
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Item packages only. MCP package names have their own widgets ("Koi Top MCP Servers", "Koi Critical/High MCP Servers").

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter package_name = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.package_name"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.package_name"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.package_name"), null))
| filter package_name != null
| comp count() as alerts by package_name
| sort desc alerts
| limit 15
| view graph type = column header = "Top Packages by Alerts" xaxis = package_name yaxis = alerts

widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Categories are item `labels[]`; MCP resources carry no labels (0/18).

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter labels_arr = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[0].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[1].labels"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_array(resources, "$[2].labels"), null))
| arrayexpand labels_arr
| alter category = json_extract_scalar(labels_arr, "$")
| filter category != null
| comp count() as alerts by category, severity
| sort desc alerts
| limit 40
| view graph type = column subtype = stacked header = "Category by Severity" xaxis = category yaxis = alerts series = severity

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"

    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.marketplace"), null),

widgets_data.title: Koi Newly Seen Items Over Time
Koi Assessment Coverage
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Coverage is measured over ITEM resources only - rows with no item risk_status are excluded from the denominator, so the 18 MCP alerts no longer drag the percentage down.

data.phrase:
dataset = koi_koi_raw
| filter source_log_type = "Alerts"
| alter item_risk_status = coalesce(
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[0].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[1].data.risk_status"), null),
    if(json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].type") = "item", json_extract_scalar(resources, "$[2].data.risk_status"), null))
| filter item_risk_status != null
| alter assessed = if(item_risk_status = "completed", 1, 0)
| comp avg(assessed) as cov
| alter coverage_pct = multiply(cov, 100)
| view graph type = single yaxis = coverage_pct scale_threshold("#00cc88") dataunit = "%"

widgets_data.title: Koi Critical-Risk Items
widgets_data.description: Item fields are read from the RAW `resources` array with a type guard (`.type = "item"`) coalesced across positions 0-2, never from a fixed `$[0]`. Live-verified 2026-07-21 on koi_koi_raw: resources[0].type is "item" on 296 alert rows but "mcp" on 18, and the MCP resource carries name/data.marketplace/data.package_name/data.risk_level/data.risk under identical keys, so an unguarded $[0] read silently reports MCP servers as items. Raw reads (not parsing-rule promoted columns) so the widget also works on data ingested before this pack was deployed. Distinct items counted from `resources[.type="item"].data.item_id`, not the promoted `item_id` column (null on already-ingested rows). Before this fix a critical filter returned 1 real item plus 2 MCP servers mislabelled as items.
